source_notes: Based on EC3 database averages for ready-mix concrete showing industry averages ranging from 298-480 kg CO2e/m3. Quantities based on basement slab (~185 m2 × 0.15m = 28 m3) plus basement walls (~66 m2 × 0.30m = 20 m3) for a typical 2,000 sq ft home.

EC3 has dimensional lumber calculators and based on literature review showing dimensional lumber typically ranges 150-200 kg CO2e/m3. Estimated 15 m3 for 2x6 framing at 16" o.c. for 2,000 sq ft two-story home including walls, floor joists, and roof framing.

Based on CORRIM EPD showing cradle-to-gate fossil GWP of ~250 kg CO2e/m3 and Chinese study showing 552 kg CO2e/m3, using mid-range estimate. Quantity includes wall sheathing (~260 m2 × 0.012m = 3.1 m3), roof sheathing (~215 m2 × 0.012m = 2.6 m3), and subfloor (~370 m2 × 0.019m = 7.0 m3), plus basement walls.

material_name: Fiberglass Batt Insulation R-21 and R-49
source_notes: Based on industry data showing fiberglass batts have 1.7-2.5 kg CO2e/m2 per inch thickness. Wall area 260 m2 × 6 inches (R-21) = 1560 m2-inch; attic area 185 m2 × 13.5 inches (R-49) = 2498 m2-inch; total 4058 m2-inch, using conservative density factor.

material_name: Asphalt Shingles for Steep Slope Residential Roofing
source_notes: Based on ARMA industry-average EPD for asphalt shingle roofing systems per m2 installed area. Estimated ~215 m2 roof area for 2,000 sq ft home with typical overhang and pitch.

Based on EC3 database range of 4-8 kg CO₂e/m² for vinyl siding referenced in industry literature. Quantity assumes 260 m² exterior wall area for 2-story home with standard overhang and gable configurations.

Based on literature review showing PVC window frames with double panes at 68 kg CO2e per functional unit plus glass components. Applied EC3 reference range of 150-250 kg CO2e/m2 for double-pane vinyl windows with 20 units at 1.4 m2 each.
